description_long: This object is a stylized representation of a hammerhead shark-shaped airplane, featuring a broad, flat front section that mimics the distinctive head of a hammerhead shark. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers smoothly toward the tail, which is equipped with a tall, vertical stabilizer and a swept-back horizontal stabilizer. The wings are mid-mounted and straight, extending outward from the body with rounded tips, and the underbelly is lighter in color, resembling the natural countershading of marine animals. The surface is smooth and matte, with subtle panel lines and minimal visible detailing, giving it a cartoonish, playful appearance. No visible engines, landing gear, or cockpit windows are present, emphasizing the object's whimsical, non-functional design. The color palette consists mainly of teal blue and white, enhancing the aquatic theme.
description: A stylized, hammerhead shark-inspired airplane with a broad, flat nose, mid-mounted straight wings, and a tall vertical tail, finished in teal blue and white.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, flat hammerhead-shaped nose and the symmetrical extension of the wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the shark-like profile, the tall vertical stabilizer, and the smooth, streamlined body.
description_view_2: Top view reveals the full wingspan, the tapering fuselage, and the distinctive hammerhead front.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the same shark-inspired features and the swept tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, broad wings and rounded tips; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single tall vertical stabilizer and swept-back horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No visible cockpit or windows; cockpit placement indeterminate.
